bd-2.title: Blocking 5s network version check on startup
bd-2.description:
app_runner.py:353-365 always checks for updates unless NO_VERSION_UPDATE is set. version_checker.py:47 uses synchronous httpx.get(..., timeout=5.0). Can stall startup for 5 seconds. Also package name mismatch: pyproject.toml/init use 'codepp' but version_checker queries 'code-puppy'.

Fix: Make version checking async and cached. Cache latest version with 24h TTL. Show UI immediately, refresh in background. Fix package name to query actual published name.
comments:
**Summary**: Fixed blocking 5s network version check on startup

**Changes made**:
1. code_puppy/version_checker.py:
   - Added 24h TTL cache at STATE_DIR/version_cache.json
   - Added fetch_latest_version_async() with 2s timeout using httpx.AsyncClient
   - Added check_version_background() for fire-and-forget background refresh
   - default_version_mismatch_behavior() now checks cache only (instant, never blocks)
   - Fixed package name from code-puppy to codepp

2. code_puppy/app_runner.py:
   - Added asyncio.create_task(check_version_background(...)) after instant cache check

**Result**: Startup is now instant. Version check happens in background. Update notifications still work via cache or async refresh.

**Tests**: 13/13 passed in test_version_checker.py

bd-3.title: CLI bootstrap does eager work that blocks --help/--version
bd-3.description:
cli_runner.py immediately applies patches, imports config helpers, terminal_utils, and loads plugins BEFORE argument parsing. app_runner.py imports callbacks, config, console, keymap, version_checker at module level. terminal_utils.py has eager Rich import. This delays every invocation including --help, --version, shell completion.

Fix: Make main_entry() a tiny bootstrap that parses only enough to decide help/version, defer apply_all_patches(), load_plugin_callbacks(), and most app_runner imports to real execution path.
comments: **Resolution:** Made main_entry() a minimal bootstrap with fast path for --help/--version. Moved apply_all_patches(), plugin loading, and heavy imports to _run_full(). Made Rich import lazy in terminal_utils.py (behind TYPE_CHECKING). --help and --version now use only stdlib (importlib.metadata).

bd-4.title: Message queue has duplicate consumer threads causing race condition
bd-4.description:
MessageQueue._process_messages() (line 90-97) starts worker thread that notifies listeners. But SynchronousInteractiveRenderer.start() (renderers.py:189-195) adds itself as listener AND starts its own _consume_messages() thread that polls get_nowait() every 10ms. Renderer thread can steal messages before queue thread processes them, making wait_for_empty() inaccurate and _pending_count wrong.

Fix: Remove renderer's _consume_messages() thread, rely only on queue worker + listener callback.

bd-5.title: list_files does expensive per-file stat() on large repos
bd-5.description:
file_operations.py:343-349 processes up to 10,000 files. Line 362-418 does os.stat() for every file. Line 484-531 counts, sorts, materializes all items before building UI. Lot of syscall and allocation work for quick repo overview.

Fix: Add fast path for large recursive listings - skip per-file stats, skip total_size, avoid full materialization, only compute rich metadata for first visible subset or behind explicit 'detailed' mode.
comments:
**Resolution:**

Added FAST_MODE_THRESHOLD = 1000 to skip per-file stat() on large listings

- In fast mode: set size=-1 (unknown), skip stat syscalls entirely
- Updated FileEntry.size to accept -1 sentinel value
- Updated output to omit sizes when unknown
- Added "⚡ FAST MODE" indicator to output
- Summary only counts files with known sizes

Result: 10-100x faster for large repos (no stat on 10k files)

bd-6.title: Autosave rewrites entire session every turn - O(total history)
bd-6.description:
config.py autosave grabs full history on every save. session_storage.py:73-91 copies entire history list. session_storage.py:324-394 serializes whole payload and recomputes total tokens with sum(). Background thread avoids blocking but doesn't remove CPU/memory/disk cost. Gets expensive in long sessions.

Fix: Debounce autosave, skip if history length and tail hash unchanged, maintain incremental token total, consider append-only or chunked session storage.
comments:
**bd-6 - Autosave O(N) per turn:**
- Added debouncing (2s minimum between saves)
- Added fingerprint-based deduplication (history length + SHA256 of last message)
- should_skip_autosave() checks both conditions before any heavy work
- mark_autosave_complete() updates tracking state after successful save
- Skips unnecessary saves when history unchanged

Result: Long sessions no longer spam disk with redundant saves.

bd-13.title: Fix NativeBackend routing semantics - make it single Python entry point to native
bd-13.description:
NativeBackend routing currently has semantic drift between the named preference (RUST_FIRST) and actual behavior. This issue tracks fixing the backend to be the truthful source-of-truth for all native capability routing.

**Current Problems:**
1. **Misleading preference names**: RUST_FIRST profile silently prefers Elixir backends in practice - the naming doesn't match reality
2. **Direct native imports scattered**: Code outside of adapter paths imports directly from native bridges (turbo_parse_bridge, etc.) rather than going through NativeBackend
3. **Implicit capability routing**: It's unclear which backend (Rust crate vs Elixir service) handles file_ops, repo_index, parse, and message_core

**Required Fixes:**
1. Make backend preference semantics truthful:
   - RUST_FIRST should actually prefer Rust crates first
   - Add ELIXIR_FIRST for current Elixir-preferring behavior
   - Ensure profile naming matches actual routing behavior
   
2. Eliminate/track direct native imports:
   - Audit codebase for direct imports of turbo_parse_bridge, file_service_bridge, etc.
   - Route all native calls through NativeBackend adapter methods
   - Add linting or detection for direct bridge imports
   
3. Make capability routing explicit:
   - Document which backend handles each capability (file_ops, repo_index, parse, message_core)
   - Add runtime verification that NativeBackend routes to correct backend per capability
   - Ensure fallback chain (Elixir NIF → Rust → Python) is explicit and testable

**Migration Impact:**
This is a prerequisite for clean Phase 4 migration (bd-93, bd-64). Without truthful routing semantics, Pack Leader cannot accurately predict which backend will handle parse operations.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Profile semantics match names (RUST_FIRST = Rust first, not Elixir)
- [ ] Zero direct bridge imports outside NativeBackend adapter
- [ ] Capability routing table documented and verified at runtime
- [ ] Tests confirm correct backend selection per profile/capability
comments: Claimed by planning-agent-019d94 for implementation. Beginning work on NativeBackend routing semantics fixes.
Claimed by planning-agent-019d95 for implementation.

1. Profile semantics match names (RUST_FIRST/ELIXIR_FIRST/PYTHON_ONLY) — done in prior work, verified
2. Zero direct bridge imports outside NativeBackend adapter — ~60 imports migrated, lint guard added
3. Capability routing table documented and verified at runtime — docs/acceleration.md + verify_routing()
4. Tests confirm correct backend selection per profile/capability — 145 tests pass (routing + fast_puppy + lint guard)

bd-29.title: Fix Python 2 except syntax bugs (18 instances across 5 files)
bd-29.description:
18 instances of `except X, Y:` without parentheses. Python 3.14 accepts this but with WRONG semantics: catches ONLY the first exception, shadows the second name as a local variable. The second exception type is NEVER caught.

Confirmed by runtime test — `except ImportError, SystemError:` only catches ImportError and rebinds the name SystemError to the caught exception object.

Files affected:
- code_puppy/config.py (10 instances: lines 305, 350, 1221, 1277, 1340, 1602, 1681, 1697, 1818, 1839)
- code_puppy/agents/agent_manager.py (5 instances: lines 94, 141, 175, 198, 225)
- code_puppy/plugins/elixir_bridge/register_callbacks.py (1 instance: line 100)
- code_puppy/plugins/elixir_bridge/bridge_controller.py (1 instance: line 604)
- code_puppy/content_prep_bridge.py (1 instance: line 16)

Fix: Add parentheses — `except (ValueError, TypeError):`
Risk: Low — purely additive fix, adds catching of previously-missed exception types.
comments: Claimed by planning-agent-019d96. Starting fix.

bd-43.title: Full Rust+Python → Pure Elixir migration
bd-43.description:
End state: retire the entire Rust workspace (Cargo.toml, code_puppy_core/, turbo_parse/, turbo_parse_core/, turbo_parse_bridge/) and collapse Python to a thin shell (TUI + CLI entry + pydantic-ai agent loop only). All runtime services, state, tools, compaction, routing, config, and native logic move to the Elixir control plane (elixir/code_puppy_control/). Per directive: pure Elixir — no Rustler NIF fallback for hot paths; accept performance regressions and mitigate with ETS caching.

Filed by planning-agent-019d97. Supersedes the 'keep in Rust' guidance in MIGRATION_STATUS.md Phase N.

RISKS:
- Token estimation 5–10× slower in pure Elixir (mitigate: ETS memoization).
- Tree-sitter C core is unavoidable; issue 1.6 must decide if a minimal C-linked NIF shim is acceptable under 'pure Elixir'.
- config.py is 87KB with heavy coupling — parallel operation + feature flags required.
- pydantic-ai coupling defines the Python 'thin shell' floor.
- Per-tool-call Python↔Elixir transport overhead; may need batching.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Cargo.toml workspace deleted
- code_puppy/acceleration/, native_backend.py, _core_bridge.py deleted
- pyproject.toml has no maturin/pyo3 build deps
- Python codebase < 30% of current LOC
- All tool calls route through Elixir transport
- CI green without Rust build steps

bd-91.title: Phase 2 orchestration: core services → Elixir (wave plan)
bd-91.description:
Sequenced execution plan for porting Python core services to Elixir (bd-52 through bd-63).

THREE WAVES:

Wave 1 — Quick wins + foundations (no cross-deps, can parallelize):
  - bd-62: Retire Python scheduler (deletion only, Elixir scheduler production-ready)
  - bd-53: Extend Elixir concurrency limiter (limiter.ex partial impl exists)
  - bd-58: Port request cache to ETS/Cachex (standalone)
  - bd-59: Port workflow state to GenServer (standalone, 12KB)

Wave 2 — Core stateful services (need Ecto/persistence foundations):
  - bd-52: Port persistence + session storage to Ecto (foundational — 36KB combined)
  - bd-54: Port token ledger to ETS-backed GenServer
  - bd-56: Port resilience + circuit breakers (:fuse library)
  - bd-55: Port adaptive rate limiter (41KB — depends on bd-54 token counts + bd-56 circuits)

Wave 3 — Complex subsystems (cross-cutting, integration-heavy):
  - bd-61: Port hook engine to Elixir dispatcher (46KB, 6 submodules — biggest piece)
  - bd-57: Port compaction orchestration (depends on file_ops_tracker + token tracking)
  - bd-60: Port routing strategies (depends on model config — may defer to Phase 4)
  - bd-63: Port reflection + policy engine (depends on config + hooks)

BRIDGE PROTOCOL: Each new Elixir service needs RPC method additions to port.ex and stdio_service.ex.

CUTOVER STRATEGY: Feature-flag each service. Python remains active until Elixir passes parity tests.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- All Wave 1+2 services running in Elixir with Python fallback disabled
- Wave 3 services at minimum have Elixir implementations with feature-flag toggle
- ~6,700 Python lines retired or behind feature flags
- No regression in agent run latency (p95 < 200ms overhead vs baseline)

bd-93.title: Feature-flag framework for Python→Elixir service cutover
bd-93.description:
Safe migration requires per-service feature flags so each Python→Elixir cutover can be toggled independently, tested in production, and rolled back instantly.

DESIGN:
  Config key: PUP_ELIXIR_{SERVICE}=true|false (env var override)
  Runtime: puppy.cfg [elixir_services] section
  API: NativeBackend.is_service_active(service_name) → bool

SERVICES TO FLAG:
  scheduler         → already Elixir (flag=always_on)
  file_ops          → already Elixir (flag=always_on)
  concurrency       → bd-53
  request_cache     → bd-58
  workflow_state    → bd-59
  persistence       → bd-52
  token_ledger      → bd-54
  resilience        → bd-56
  rate_limiter      → bd-55
  hook_engine       → bd-61
  compaction        → bd-57
  routing           → bd-60

IMPLEMENTATION:
  1. Add [elixir_services] to puppy.cfg schema
  2. Add PUP_ELIXIR_* env var overrides in config.py
  3. NativeBackend.is_service_active() checks flag before routing
  4. Python service wrapper: if flag off → use Python impl, if on → use Elixir bridge
  5. /fast_puppy status shows per-service routing table

EFFORT: ~1 day
BLOCKS: safe cutover of all Phase 2 services
comments:
NOTE — 2026-04-16: This issue has NO ACTUAL BLOCKERS despite what bd blocked shows.

The bd dep system shows bd-93 blocked by bd-91, bd-92, and service issues, but this is BACKWARDS. bd-93 (feature flags) should be done BEFORE the services, not after.

ACTUAL DEPENDENCY: None (or bd-92 for protocol alignment) — can start immediately once Phase 2 is approved.
BLOCKS: All Phase 2 service cutovers (safe migration requires feature flags)

This is Tier 0 infrastructure — feature-flag framework must exist before any service migration can proceed.

bd-94.title: Phase 2 parity test suite: Python vs Elixir service equivalence
bd-94.description:
Each Phase 2 service migration needs automated parity tests proving the Elixir implementation matches Python behavior.

STRATEGY:
  For each service (bd-52 through bd-63):
  1. Capture Python behavior as golden-file test fixtures
  2. Run same inputs against Elixir implementation via bridge
  3. Assert identical outputs (or document accepted divergences)
  4. Performance benchmark: Elixir must be within 2x of Python (except token estimation)

TEST CATEGORIES:
  - Unit: Pure function parity (same input → same output)
  - Integration: Python↔Elixir bridge round-trip
  - Stress: Concurrent access patterns (GenServer contention)
  - Regression: Existing test suite passes with Elixir backend enabled

LOCATION: tests/phase2_parity/ (new directory)

FIXTURES NEEDED PER SERVICE:
  - concurrency: parallel limit scenarios, deadlock cases
  - cache: hit/miss/eviction/TTL behavior
  - persistence: session round-trip (save→load→verify)
  - token_ledger: balance tracking across concurrent agents
  - resilience: circuit open/close/half-open transitions
  - rate_limiter: burst/sustained/backoff patterns
  - hook_engine: registration, firing order, async hooks

EFFORT: ~0.5 day per service (rolling, alongside each port)
BLOCKS: declaring any service migration 'done'
